metadata.summary: GA4 setup, event taxonomy, custom dimensions, Data API reporting, and Measurement Protocol integration with agent rules for parameter limits and data consistency.

content.title: Google Analytics 4 (GA4)
content.summary: GA4 setup, event taxonomy, custom dimensions, Data API reporting, and framework integrations (React/Gatsby, Django, Measurement Protocol). The rule: lock the event taxonomy in a repo file before deploying code; register all custom dimensions in GA4 Admin before data flows; always pass client_id from the cookie when sending Measurement Protocol events server-side.

why.p: GA4 is free and ubiquitous for web/app analytics, but its event-driven model requires upfront taxonomy design. Silent data loss is the most common failure: events with unregistered custom dimensions write nothing; Measurement Protocol calls with fresh UUIDs as client_id create ghost users; custom dimension names exceeding 40 characters are silently truncated. An agent that generates the taxonomy, code, and admin checklist in one pass prevents all three.

list.item: Instrumenting a new site/app and needing a complete event taxonomy + gtag/Measurement Protocol code.
Agent-driven daily or weekly reporting via the Data API.
Auditing an existing GA4 property for misconfigurations (missing user_id, broken funnels, double-counted events).
Server-side tracking via Measurement Protocol because client tracking is blocked by ad-blockers.
Privacy-strict EU-only audiences without server-side proxy + Consent Mode — GA4 in default mode breaks GDPR/Schrems II for many setups; use Plausible or Matomo instead.
High-volume product analytics needing sub-second latency — GA4 data is sampled and delayed; use Mixpanel, Amplitude, or PostHog.
BigQuery export not enabled — agent analytical depth is capped at the GA4 UI.
B2B sales-led operations where revenue is closed in CRM, not on the site.

section.name: data-streams

rule.statement: Create one data stream per platform (web, iOS, Android); use a single GA4 property to unify cross-platform data rather than separate properties per platform.
rule.rationale: Separate properties prevent cross-platform user stitching. A unified property with multiple streams allows unified user_id tracking and cross-platform funnel analysis.

section.reference: GA4 gtag.js installation snippet for web streams

section.name: enhanced-measurement
section.p: When enabled, GA4 auto-tracks: page_view, scroll (90% depth), outbound link clicks, site search, video engagement, and file downloads. These require no code but can be selectively disabled.

rule.statement: Enable BigQuery export from day 1 — it is free for most traffic volumes and bypasses sampling and thresholding that affects the GA4 UI.
rule.rationale: Without BigQuery, agents are limited to sampled, rounded UI data. BigQuery gives raw event-level access, enabling accurate cohort, funnel, and retention queries.

rule.statement: Set user properties (subscription_tier, signup_source) via gtag('set', 'user_properties', {...}) before sending events so that all subsequent events in the session carry the properties.
rule.rationale: User properties must be set before the events they should segment. Setting them after a session starts means those events have null property values in reports.

list.item: Automatic: collected by default — first_visit, session_start, page_view
Enhanced measurement: scroll, click (outbound), file_download, video_start/complete
Recommended: Google-defined schemas — login, sign_up, purchase, begin_checkout, add_to_cart
Custom: product-specific — feature_used, subscription_started, error_occurred

rule.statement: Prefer recommended event names (login, sign_up, purchase) over custom names when the semantic is identical; Google pre-configures reports and conversion tracking for them.
rule.rationale: Custom event names require manual report configuration. Recommended events flow into built-in reports and conversion funnels automatically.

example.title: Key Recommended Events

rule.statement: Keep event names under 40 characters and parameter names under 40 characters; values under 100 characters; max 25 parameters per event. Build a validator into the codegen step.
rule.rationale: GA4 silently truncates or drops data that exceeds these limits. There is no error — data just disappears. Agents auto-generating long names from product specs hit this constantly.

rule.statement: Register all custom dimensions in GA4 Admin (Admin → Data display → Custom definitions) BEFORE deploying code that sends them; unregistered params are collected but invisible in reports.
rule.rationale: GA4 stores raw event params but only surfaces registered custom dimensions in the UI and Data API. Events sent before registration are not backfilled.

section.name: data-api-reporting
section.reference: Python Data API helper for daily top-events report

list.item: Common dimensions: date, eventName, pagePath, sessionSource, deviceCategory, country
Common metrics: eventCount, totalUsers, sessions, averageSessionDuration, bounceRate, conversions
API quota: 50 concurrent requests, 250k tokens/day per property — a haiku agent in a tight loop can exhaust this

rule.statement: When sending Measurement Protocol events server-side, always pass the client_id from the browser cookie (_ga cookie value); never generate a fresh UUID server-side for existing users.
rule.rationale: A server-side call with a fresh UUID creates a ghost user in GA4 that is counted separately from the real user. This inflates user counts and breaks funnel attribution.

antipattern.description: purchase event fires without transaction_id parameter.
antipattern.reason: Without transaction_id, GA4 cannot deduplicate purchase events. If the page reloads or the event fires twice, revenue is double-counted. Always include transaction_id in every purchase event.

antipattern.description: Agent generates event/parameter names longer than 40 characters from product specs.
antipattern.reason: GA4 silently truncates names. Build a validator into the taxonomy codegen step: assert len(name) <= 40 for every event and parameter name before deploying.

antipattern.description: DebugView used to confirm historical events are tracking correctly.
antipattern.reason: DebugView only shows real-time events. Standard reports have a 24-48h lag. An agent asking "why isn't my event in reports?" must wait, not re-debug the implementation.

antipattern.description: Service account JSON keys passed as a parameter in agent prompts.
antipattern.reason: JSON keys in prompts leak credentials into LLM context logs. Use Workload Identity Federation or short-lived OAuth tokens; never hard-code key material.

antipattern.description: Comparing pre-Consent-Mode vs post-Consent-Mode periods as a performance regression.
antipattern.reason: Consent Mode v2 changes reported data dramatically (EU traffic can drop 30-50%). An agent must check consent mode rollout dates before attributing declines to campaign performance.

antipattern.description: Agent-generated single-domain gtag code deployed on a multi-domain site.
antipattern.reason: Without the linker config (gtag 'linker' parameter), users crossing domains appear as new sessions with lost attribution. Always check if the site spans multiple domains before generating gtag code.

rule.statement: "Conversions" in GA4 reports only counts events that were explicitly marked as conversions in Admin; an agent reporting "conversions" from a property where no events are marked returns zero.
rule.rationale: GA4 does not automatically infer conversion intent from event names. An agent must verify which events are marked as conversions in Admin before interpreting conversion metrics.

template.purpose: GA4 gtag.js installation snippet for web streams
Python Data API helper for daily top-events report

section.name: best-practices
section.p: Lock the event taxonomy in an events.md file in the repo and have agents diff against it before code changes. Always pass client_id from cookie when sending Measurement Protocol events; never let the agent generate fresh UUIDs server-side. Enable BigQuery export from day 1 — it's free for most volumes and bypasses sampling/thresholding. Use GTM Server-Side for resilience against ad-blockers and to keep secrets server-side. Register all custom dimensions in admin before deploying code that sends them; agents should generate the admin checklist alongside the code. Tie consent-mode signals to the event flow; without it, post-2024 EU traffic is severely under-reported.

section.name: ai-agent-gotchas-extended
section.p: Agents may exceed the 25-parameter or 40-char-name limits silently; build a validator into the codegen step. The Data API has quotas (50 concurrent, 250k tokens/day per property); a haiku agent in a tight loop can exhaust them. DebugView only shows real-time, not historical; agents asking "why isn't my event in standard reports?" must remember the 24-48h lag. Service account JSON keys leak into prompts — use Workload Identity Federation or short-lived OAuth tokens. Cross-domain tracking requires the linker config; agent-generated single-domain code breaks attribution on multi-domain sites. "Conversions" in GA4 = events you marked as conversions in admin; an agent reporting "conversions" without that mapping reports zero.

section.name: agentic-workflow
section.p: A sonnet instrumentation agent generates a full event taxonomy (recommended events, custom events, params, user properties) from a product spec, plus the gtag and Measurement Protocol code. A haiku reporting agent runs daily Data API queries and writes a markdown digest (top events, top sources, conversion deltas). An opus audit agent inspects the property for missing key events, broken funnels, BigQuery export status, and consent-mode coverage.

list.item: faion-growth-agent (opus for audits, sonnet for taxonomy design).
Generic data/haiku subagent — daily Data API pulls into markdown.
DevTools/sonnet subagent — write/refactor gtag, GTM tags, server-side MP calls.

section.name: prompt-pattern
section.p: Pattern 1: "Given product = {description} with key flows {flow_list}, generate a GA4 event taxonomy: recommended events (login/sign_up/purchase) and custom events. For each, list params (≤25, ≤40 chars), user_properties, and admin steps to register custom dimensions. Output as markdown table." Pattern 2: "Pull last 7d from property {id}: top 10 events by count, top 5 sources, conversion count by event_name. Use Data API with date_ranges [7daysAgo, today]. Return markdown + flag any event with day-over-day delta >30%."

section.name: cli-tools-and-services
section.p: CLI tools include google-analytics-data Python SDK (query Data API reports; pip install google-analytics-data), google-analytics-admin Python SDK (manage properties, custom dimensions; pip install google-analytics-admin), gcloud CLI (auth, service account keys for API access; https://cloud.google.com/sdk), GTM Server-Side container (server-side tagging proxy; https://developers.google.com/tag-platform/tag-manager/server-side), bq CLI (query GA4 BigQuery export tables; https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/bq-command-line-tool), and dbt (model GA4 BigQuery export into clean tables; pip install dbt-bigquery). Services include GA4 Data API (SaaS, read-only reporting, yes REST + SDKs), GA4 Admin API (SaaS, manage events, audiences, custom dims, yes REST), GA4 Measurement Protocol (SaaS, server-side event ingest, yes REST), BigQuery (GA4 export) (SaaS, raw event-level data, no sampling, yes SQL + bq), Looker Studio (SaaS, dashboards, partial APIs limited, agent can build via gas), GTM (web + server) (SaaS, partial container management API exists but heavy), Stape / Addingwell (SaaS, yes managed server-side GTM hosting), and Consent platforms (CookieYes, OneTrust, Cookiebot) (SaaS, yes REST, required for GA4 + EU compliance).

section.name: inline-data-api-helper
section.p: Inline Data API helper for agent's daily report: from google.analytics.data_v1beta import BetaAnalyticsDataClient; from google.analytics.data_v1beta.types import DateRange, Dimension, Metric, RunReportRequest. def top_events(property_id: str, days: int = 7): client = BetaAnalyticsDataClient(); req = RunReportRequest(property=f"properties/{property_id}", date_ranges=[DateRange(start_date=f"{days}daysAgo", end_date="today")], dimensions=[Dimension(name="eventName")], metrics=[Metric(name="eventCount"), Metric(name="totalUsers")], limit=10); resp = client.run_report(req); return [{"event": r.dimension_values[0].value, "count": int(r.metric_values[0].value), "users": int(r.metric_values[1].value)} for r in resp.rows].

list.item: GA4 Data API docs — https://developers.google.com/analytics/devguides/reporting/data/v1
Measurement Protocol GA4 — https://developers.google.com/analytics/devguides/collection/protocol/ga4
Simo Ahava's blog — https://www.simoahava.com/ (de facto practitioner reference)
GA4 BigQuery export schema — https://support.google.com/analytics/answer/7029846
Google's GA4 migration guide — https://support.google.com/analytics/topic/14088998
